without_skill.evidence: The resource-level comment is immediately followed by "---" with no blank line, and the section comment is immediately followed by [inbox.notifications] with no blank line, so both attach correctly.

with_skill.evidence: A blank line separates the resource-level comment from the "---" frontmatter marker, which breaks attachment under the proposal's rules, leaving the comment orphaned.

without_skill.evidence: Uses [inbox.notifications] correctly, but the count entry has no key at all — the .input/.match lines follow directly with no "count =" declaration, so count is not a valid entry.

with_skill.evidence: Never emits a real [inbox.notifications] section-head; instead flattens the section into dotted entry ids (inbox.notifications.title, inbox.notifications.count), so there is no section as such.

without_skill.evidence: The count value's .input/.match/variant lines are flush-left with no indentation and have no preceding key, so it is not a valid multiline entry value.

with_skill.evidence: count entry's continuation lines are indented two spaces, and the embedded message is a valid MF2 matcher with a * fallback.

without_skill.evidence: Uses a plain # comment ("# $count: the number of unread notifications...") instead of @param metadata for $count.

with_skill.evidence: Provides an overall inbox comment and an explicit "@param $count - Number of unread notifications in the inbox." line directly before the count entry.

without_skill.evidence: Incorrectly claims "the container format has no document-separator token" and that any use of --- is invalid, rather than diagnosing that frontmatter cannot follow an entry; the corrected resource simply drops the marker under that wrong premise.

with_skill.evidence: Correctly identifies that the entry before "---" is the problem ("welcome = Hello appears before the --- frontmatter marker... forbids any entry... before frontmatter") and fixes it by moving the entry after frontmatter.

without_skill.evidence: Never discusses the blank line after @owner or any attachment failure; @owner is silently kept where it was without analysis.

with_skill.evidence: Explains "@owner localization is followed by an empty line... As written, @owner attaches to nothing", frames it as an attachment issue rather than an ABNF violation, and relocates it before frontmatter so it attaches to the whole resource.

without_skill.evidence: Fixes bad?key by deleting the question mark outright (badkey = Oops), changing the key's literal identity instead of escaping it.

with_skill.evidence: Escapes the key as bad\x3fkey = Oops, preserving its literal identity.

without_skill.evidence: Corrected resource indents all continuation lines of items by two spaces.

with_skill.evidence: Corrected resource indents all continuation lines of items by two spaces.

without_skill.evidence: States "Embedded MF2 error: none. The message body itself is well-formed MF2", separating it from the container fixes.

with_skill.evidence: States "Embedded MF2 error: none... the items message body itself is valid MF2", separating it from the container fixes.

without_skill.evidence: The key "Are you sure?" = {{Continue?}} uses a quoted string as an identifier, which the id grammar (id-safe = ALPHA / DIGIT / "_", with hex-escapes for other characters) does not permit, so the resource is not grammatically valid.

with_skill.evidence: Every entry (plain.title, items, separator, are_you_sure\x3f) uses a grammatically valid identifier and a valid MF2 value.

without_skill.evidence: plain.title correctly uses a structural dot, but the source key's question mark is left inside an unescaped quoted-string identifier ("Are you sure?") instead of a hex-escaped id like are_you_sure\x3f.

with_skill.evidence: plain.title uses a structural dot, and are_you_sure\x3f hex-escapes the literal question mark from the source key.

without_skill.evidence: items declares .input {$count :integer}, matches $count with one/* variants preserving singular/plural meaning.

with_skill.evidence: items declares .input {$count :integer}, matches $count with one/* variants preserving singular/plural meaning.

without_skill.evidence: separator = {{, }} is a quoted pattern that resolves to comma-plus-space with no literal bar characters.

with_skill.evidence: separator = |, | places a quoted literal delimiter directly as a bare simple pattern, which (per the skill's own authoring reference) renders the pipe characters literally instead of resolving to comma-plus-space.

without_skill.evidence: Comments explain each entry's purpose without obscuring the resource syntax.

with_skill.evidence: Comments and @param metadata explain each entry's purpose without obscuring the resource syntax.
